trajectory_capture_note: Captured provider-exposed CLI output and structured events when available. Private chain-of-thought reasoning is not available from these adapters.

trajectory.result:
`decide.py` is complete and all 15 oracle-backed examples pass. Here is a summary of what was implemented:

**Structure of `decide.py`**

- `realcompare(a, b)` — exact copy of the reference semantics: tolerance-based six-significant-digit comparison returning `"LT"`, `"EQ"`, or `"GT"`.
- Geometry helpers used throughout: `_dist`, `_circumradius` (handles collinear case via maximum-diameter circle), `_triangle_area`, `_angle` (returns `None` when either endpoint coincides with the vertex), `_quadrant`, `_dist_point_to_line`.
- 15 LIC evaluators (`_lic0` … `_lic14`, numbered from 0 to align with Python list indices):
  - LIC 0 — consecutive pair distance > LENGTH1
  - LIC 1 — three consecutive points not fitting in circle of RADIUS1
  - LIC 2 — angle at vertex outside `[PI-EPSILON, PI+EPSILON]`
  - LIC 3 — triangle area > AREA1
  - LIC 4 — Q_PTS consecutive points in more than QUADS quadrants (with spec tie-break priority)
  - LIC 5 — consecutive X decrease
  - LIC 6 — N_PTS window with a point > DIST from the first-to-last line (coincident-endpoint case handled)
  - LIC 7 — K_PTS-separated pair distance > LENGTH1
  - LIC 8 — A_PTS/B_PTS-separated triple not fitting in RADIUS1 circle
  - LIC 9 — C_PTS/D_PTS-separated triple angle outside range
  - LIC 10 — E_PTS/F_PTS-separated triple area > AREA1
  - LIC 11 — G_PTS-separated X decrease
  - LIC 12 — two-part: K_PTS pair > LENGTH1 AND < LENGTH2
  - LIC 13 — two-part: A_PTS/B_PTS triple outside RADIUS1 AND inside RADIUS2
  - LIC 14 — two-part: E_PTS/F_PTS triple area > AREA1 AND < AREA2
- `decide(numpoints, x, y, parameters, lcm, pum_diag)` — assembles CMV, PUM (diagonal from `pum_diag`, off-diagonal from LCM×CMV), FUV, and the launch decision.
- `main()` — reads one JSON object from stdin, calls `decide`, writes the result to stdout.
